correctness.notes: Assumption ass:nuisance as consumed by Theorem thm:feasible-upper-log / label conj:upper-rate: The proof closes the estimated-clipping variance term by assuming the estimator-determined bound ||ehat-e_P||_{2,P_X} <= C rho_n with rho_n=bar rho_{n,L2}^*, plus local outcome L1 and q_n^{-1}-weighted product bounds at the same target scale. These are not derived from primitive smoothness, known-logging, sample-size, tail, or standard nuisance-rate assumptions. In regimes such as beta_{alpha,gamma}<alpha, the required propensity L2 exponent r_*=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta) exceeds 1/2, so an unknown logging propensity would need faster-than-root-n uniform L2 accuracy; the note supplies no primitive condition under which that is achievable.

perItemFindings.label: correctness
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] Assumption ass:nuisance as consumed by Theorem thm:feasible-upper-log / label conj:upper-rate: The proof closes the estimated-clipping variance term by assuming the estimator-determined bound ||ehat-e_P||_{2,P_X} <= C rho_n with rho_n=bar rho_{n,L2}^*, plus local outcome L1 and q_n^{-1}-weighted product bounds at the same target scale. These are not derived from primitive smoothness, known-logging, sample-size, tail, or standard nuisance-rate assumptions. In regimes such as beta_{alpha,gamma}<alpha, the required propensity L2 exponent r_*=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta) exceeds 1/2, so an unknown logging propensity would need faster-than-root-n uniform L2 accuracy; the note supplies no primitive condition under which that is achievable. → The displayed upper rate and the feasible bracket are only conditional on assuming the estimator already attains the rate needed to control the extra q^{-2} int_D |ehat-e_P| dP_X term. The headline data-only feasible achievability claim must be discharged from primitive learnability conditions or re-scoped to a primitive known-logging/sufficiently structured regime where the nuisance bounds actually hold.

verbatim_critique:
D0.5.1 (math referee) found load-bearing correctness defects — re-derive the flagged step(s):
[serious, setup] Assumption ass:nuisance as consumed by Theorem thm:feasible-upper-log / label conj:upper-rate: The proof closes the estimated-clipping variance term by assuming the estimator-determined bound ||ehat-e_P||_{2,P_X} <= C rho_n with rho_n=bar rho_{n,L2}^*, plus local outcome L1 and q_n^{-1}-weighted product bounds at the same target scale. These are not derived from primitive smoothness, known-logging, sample-size, tail, or standard nuisance-rate assumptions. In regimes such as beta_{alpha,gamma}<alpha, the required propensity L2 exponent r_*=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta) exceeds 1/2, so an unknown logging propensity would need faster-than-root-n uniform L2 accuracy; the note supplies no primitive condition under which that is achievable.. Fix: The displayed upper rate and the feasible bracket are only conditional on assuming the estimator already attains the rate needed to control the extra q^{-2} int_D |ehat-e_P| dP_X term. The headline data-only feasible achievability claim must be discharged from primitive learnability conditions or re-scoped to a primitive known-logging/sufficiently structured regime where the nuisance bounds actually hold.

Referee notes: I reproduced the upper drift/variance fixed point, the weak-overlap two-point converse, and the feasible-bracket transfer. I found no dropped algebraic first-order term, but the upper/bracket rate relies on an undisclosed estimator-side nuisance-rate assumption rather than primitive achievable conditions.
